status: internal commercial language audit, not buyer-ready final copy
overall_verdict: The current draft accurately captures SpecShift's core technical mechanics but suffers from positioning leakage. It borrows vocabulary from cybersecurity, active runtime monitoring, and formal compliance frameworks. The framing should pivot from active containment and enforcement to pre-deployment diagnostic mapping and structural risk insulation.
strongest_buyer_value: Identifies structural logic failures and epistemic leaks in complex agent chains before they reach production.
Reduces enterprise integration rework costs by stress-testing systemic boundaries against non-deterministic inputs.
Provides operational maps of where an agent workflow's automated logic stops fitting, establishing defensible human-in-the-loop transition baselines.
overclaim_risks: Boundary Containment implies active, inline runtime mitigation.
Lineage Integrity may imply automated cryptographic data-provenance tracking software.
Authority may imply active modification or enforcement of IAM permissions.
underclaim_risks: Reports and maps may sound like static consulting shelfware unless framed as repeatable diagnostic outputs.
The strict pre-deployment boundary may sound too weak unless framed as architecture-stage risk reduction.
buyer_confusion_risks: Risk buyers may mistake this for regulatory compliance checking.
Enterprise architects may confuse state synchronization with ETL or database replication tooling.
HR and legal buyers may view workflow authority assessment as employee surveillance or performance management.

language_to_replace.risk: Sounds like active runtime control or command-and-control infrastructure.
Implies real-time cybersecurity firewall or active mitigation.
Can be confused with IAM software or HR authority structures.
Sounds like database or data infrastructure synchronization.

module_review.risk_flag: Can be mistaken for continuous data-governance software unless framed as architecture-stage review.
Low risk if not framed as complete edge-case discovery.
High risk of sounding like permission-system or infrastructure tooling.
Critical risk because containment implies production runtime intervention.

deliverable_review.risk_flag: Tie explicitly to architecture-stage design review.
Specify propensity-for-drift review, not live monitoring.
Avoid suggesting automatic runtime routing configuration.

recommended_positioning: SpecShift should be positioned as a Pre-Deployment Architectural Logic Stress-Testing and Constraint Mapping Framework for Agentic Workflows.
failure_column: This commercial review framework stops fitting if a buyer's core requirement is an inline, real-time security intervention tool, or if procurement requires verified, industry-standard legal compliance metrics before contract signing.
final_recommendation: Strip active runtime verbs such as contain, enforce, and synchronize from public product language. Reframe modules as diagnostic discovery and architecture-stage review outputs.
